decks.notes: The deck focuses on quickly and efficiently casting a large number of spells in order to quickly win the game. It utilizes cards like Lotus Petal, Simian Spirit Guide, and Dark Ritual to generate mana early on, along with multiple ways to fill the graveyard such as Poxwalkers and Balustrade Spy. Once the graveyard is full, the deck aims to use cards like Dread Return to bring back key creatures like Thassa's Oracle to win the game on the spot. The deck also includes disruption and protection in the form of Pact of Negation and Cabal Therapy.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to control the game and lock down the opponent's options. It utilizes cards like Trinisphere and Karn, the Great Creator to disrupt the opponent's plays, while also using cards like Planar Nexus and Ugin, Eye of the Storms to maintain control of the game. The deck also has powerful win conditions such as Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and The One Ring. Overall, the key concept of the deck is to establish a prison-like control over the game using artifacts and powerful spells.

The key strategy of this Mardu Aggro deck is to aggressively control the battlefield using creatures like Amped Raptor and Guide of Souls, while also disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy. The deck also utilizes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and has a sacrifice outlet in Goblin Bombardment for additional reach. The mana base includes a mix of fetch lands and dual lands to ensure smooth mana production. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early, disrupt the opponent's plans, and close out the game quickly.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly generate mana and combo off with Necrodominance and Tendrils of Agony to deal a large amount of damage to the opponent in a single turn. The deck focuses on using fast mana sources like Simian Spirit Guide, Lotus Petal, and Gemstone Mine to ramp into its combo pieces efficiently. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Chrome Mox, and Cabal Ritual, the deck can generate a lot of mana in a short amount of time to execute its game-winning combo.

This deck focuses on using disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Daze to control the game while efficiently deploying threats like Murktide Regent and Brazen Borrower to pressure the opponent. The combination of countermagic and removal spells in conjunction with powerful creatures allows the deck to stay ahead on tempo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The lands are tailored to support the aggressive playstyle, with Wasteland for disruption and access to all necessary colors for the spells in the deck. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while presenting a fast clock to secure a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly get powerful creatures onto the battlefield by utilizing the card "Show and Tell" in combination with cards like "Sneak Attack" and "Omniscience". By cheating out high-cost creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Griselbrand, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with massive threats and win the game in just a few turns. The deck also includes counterspells like "Force of Will" and disruption cards like "Flusterstorm" to protect its combo pieces and ensure a successful showcase of its powerful creatures.

This deck focuses on locking down the opponent's resources and disrupting their game plan by using artifacts to create a prison-like board state. Key cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms, Trinisphere, and Karn, the Great Creator prevent the opponent from casting spells efficiently. The combination of powerful artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and The One Ring, along with mana acceleration from Urza lands and Grim Monolith, allow for a strong control strategy. The deck also includes win conditions like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn to close out the game.

This UWx Control deck focuses on utilizing a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. With cards like Force of Will, Stifle, Swords to Plowshares, and Teferi, Time Raveler, the deck aims to protect its own threats while efficiently dealing with the opponent's threats. The deck also includes card filtering with Brainstorm and Ponder, as well as powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, to provide additional value and card advantage. The control elements of the deck allow it to dominate the game and dictate the pace of play.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive spells to maintain control over the game while applying pressure to the opponent. By casting cheap threats like Barrowgoyf and Orcish Bowmasters early on and protecting them with countermagic like Force of Will and Daze, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and quickly close out the game. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Murktide Regent offer additional card advantage and threats, while removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out deal with opposing creatures. The deck is able to maintain a steady tempo while gradually overpowering the opponent.

The main concept of this UWx Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage engines. By using cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Stifle to interact with the opponent's threats, and cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to generate card advantage and control the board, this deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind them out over the course of the game while protecting its own win conditions.

This deck focuses on quickly and efficiently reanimating powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty, Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and Griselbrand using cards like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Unmask, Thoughtseize, and Collective Brutality to protect its combos and disrupt the opponent's plans. By utilizing graveyard manipulation and powerful reanimation spells, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its big creatures and close out the game quickly. It also includes some unique reanimation targets like Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers and Valgavoth, Terror Eater to add versatility to its game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly casting a multitude of spells to combo off and win the game in a single turn. It uses cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate mana, while also utilizing cards like Pact of Negation and Cabal Therapy for protection and disruption. The main win condition involves milling out the opponent's library with Balustrade Spy or Undercity Informer, and then using Dread Return to bring back Thassa's Oracle for the win. With minimal interaction and a high risk-high reward strategy, the deck aims to win before the opponent can stop it.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful Storm combo to quickly overwhelm opponents. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Lion's Eye Diamond, and Infernal Tutor, the deck aims to generate massive amounts of mana and spells in a single turn to cast Tendrils of Agony for lethal damage. Additionally, cards like Wishclaw Talisman and Burning Wish provide flexibility and access to key combo pieces or answers when needed. The deck seeks to outpace opponents by efficiently chaining spells together to achieve victory in a single explosive turn.

The primary strategy of this deck revolves around utilizing cards like Show and Tell, Phyrexian Metamorph, and Copy Artifact to quickly bring out powerful artifacts like Coveted Jewel and Grim Monolith. By generating mana efficiently and using cards like Paradox Engine and The One Ring, the deck aims to assemble a strong board state and disrupt the opponent with cards like Force of Will. With access to powerful finishers like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with its combo potential and win the game quickly.

This deck aims to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, then bring them back into play with cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead. Using cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, the deck can rapidly cast spells and disrupt the opponent's hand with Thoughtseize and Unmask. It also contains versatile removal options like Collective Brutality and Cabal Therapy. The key strategy is to efficiently reanimate big threats and overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to respond.

This deck focuses on deploying aggressive creatures quickly and efficiently to overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards like Seasoned Dungeoneer, Caves of Chaos Adventurer, and Undermountain Adventurer, the strategy is to establish board presence and maintain control. Chancellor of the Annex provides disruption for the opponent's spells, while cards like Lotus Petal and Chrome Mox help accelerate the deck's game plan. Anointed Peacekeeper and Solitude provide additional protection and removal options. The deck aims to seize the initiative early on and push for a quick victory.
